Class InventoryManager

  • java.lang.Object
    • InventoryManager

  • public class InventoryManager
extends java.lang.Object
    Drew Carrillo CEN 3024C 04/13/2026 CLASS NAME: InventoryManager This is the business logic layer of the application. It handles all the database operations for the video game inventory. Role in the system: - Sits between the GUI and the SQLite database - Handles all CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete) - Does the custom calculation for total inventory value - Has safety checks so the app doesn't crash if someone clicks buttons before connecting to the database Dependencies: - Uses VideoGame class to create/return game objects - Connects to any SQLite database file the user provides

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      InventoryManager()
      Constructor for InventoryManager.

      Calculates the total dollar value of all games combined.

      Connects to an SQLite database file.

      Builds a formatted string of all games for the text area.

      Looks up a single game by its ID.

      Grabs every game from the database and puts them in a list.

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • InventoryManager

        public InventoryManager()
        Constructor for InventoryManager. Purpose: Sets up the manager without connecting to anything yet. The database connection gets created later when the user clicks "Load DB".

    • Method Detail

      • connectToDatabase

        public boolean connectToDatabase(java.lang.String filepath)
        Connects to an SQLite database file. Purpose: Lets the user pick their own database file instead of hardcoding one. This way the professor can test with their own file.
        Parameters:
        filepath - Path to the database file (like "inventory.db")
        Returns:
        true if connection worked, false if something went wrong

      • getAllGames

        public java.util.ArrayList<VideoGame> getAllGames()
        Grabs every game from the database and puts them in a list. Purpose: Replaces the old text file reading. This pulls data straight from the database and turns each row into a VideoGame object.
        Returns:
        An ArrayList of all VideoGame objects in the database

      • addGame

        public boolean addGame(VideoGame game)
        Adds a new game to the database. Purpose: Inserts a new row into the games table. Won't add duplicates (checks if the ID already exists).
        Parameters:
        game - The VideoGame object to add
        Returns:
        true if it was added, false if ID already exists or DB error

      • removeGame

        public boolean removeGame(int id)
        Deletes a game from the database using its ID. Purpose: Removes the row that matches the given ID. Does nothing if the ID isn't found.
        Parameters:
        id - The ID of the game to remove
        Returns:
        true if deleted successfully, false if not found or DB error

      • updateGame

        public boolean updateGame(VideoGame game)
        Updates an existing game's info in the database. Purpose: Changes all the fields for a game that already exists. Uses the ID to find which row to update.
        Parameters:
        game - The VideoGame object with the updated information
        Returns:
        true if update worked, false if error

      • displayInventory

        public java.lang.String displayInventory()
        Builds a formatted string of all games for the text area. Purpose: Gets all games and formats them so they look nice when displayed in the GUI. Shows a message if not connected.
        Returns:
        A multi-line string with all game info, or an error message

      • calculateTotalValue

        public double calculateTotalValue()
        Calculates the total dollar value of all games combined. Purpose: Uses a SQL SUM query to add up all the prices. Way faster than looping through everything in Java.
        Returns:
        The total value as a double (returns 0.0 if not connected or error)

      • findGameById

        public VideoGame findGameById(int id)
        Looks up a single game by its ID. Purpose: Used internally when adding/deleting to check if a game exists. Also useful for the GUI's update feature.
        Parameters:
        id - The ID to search for
        Returns:
        The VideoGame object if found, or null if not found
